topic: Effects of pressure and temperature on particle motion

questions.topic: Effects of pressure and temperature on particle motion
questions.question_summary: Explains the decrease in gas pressure inside a syringe when the volume is increased at a constant temperature by relating it to the frequency of particle collisions.
questions.reasoning_focus: Explaining macroscopic pressure changes through microscopic particle behavior on container walls.

questions.topic: Effects of pressure and temperature on particle motion
questions.question_summary: Compares the separation and speed of particles in a hot gas versus a cool liquid maintained at the same pressure.
questions.reasoning_focus: Identifying how higher temperature increases particle speed and how gas state increases particle separation.

questions.topic: Effects of pressure and temperature on particle motion
questions.question_summary: Calculates the new pressure of a fixed mass of gas when its volume expands from 1.2 m³ to 1.8 m³ at constant temperature.
questions.reasoning_focus: Applying simple quantitative relationships between gas pressure and volume.

questions.topic: Effects of pressure and temperature on particle motion
questions.question_summary: Explains why heating a gas in a sealed container causes the pressure to increase by focusing on the impact force of particles.
questions.reasoning_focus: Connecting increased thermal energy to the force with which gas particles strike container walls.
